attributes.stem: A 28-year-old woman presents to the antenatal clinic at 32 weeks gestation. This is her first pregnancy and she has no significant medical history. She does not take any regular medications. She is a non-smoker and works as an accountant. On examination, she appears well with vital signs within normal limits. Her symphysis-fundal height measures 28 cm. A growth scan two weeks ago showed the estimated fetal weight on the 7th centile.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most accurate method of diagnosing intrauterine growth restriction?
options: Serial measurements of fetal abdominal circumference
Serial measurements of amniotic fluid index
Serial measurements of estimated fetal weight
attributes.explanation:
This patient presents with clinical and ultrasound findings suggestive of a small-for-gestational-age fetus, which requires careful monitoring to distinguish between constitutional smallness and pathological intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR).

Serial measurements of fetal abdominal circumference is the most accurate single parameter for diagnosing IUGR. The abdominal circumference reflects liver size and subcutaneous fat deposition, making it the most sensitive indicator of fetal nutrition and growth. A declining growth velocity on serial measurements is more significant than a single measurement below the 10th centile.

Serial measurements of estimated fetal weight can be useful but is less accurate as it combines multiple measurements (head circumference, abdominal circumference, and femur length), each with their own margin of error. The composite nature of this measurement makes it less sensitive to growth restriction than abdominal circumference alone.

Serial measurements of biparietal diameter and head circumference are less useful for diagnosing IUGR as head growth is often preserved in growth-restricted fetuses due to brain-sparing effects. These measurements are more useful for dating pregnancies and assessing symmetric versus asymmetric growth restriction.

Serial measurements of amniotic fluid index may be reduced in IUGR due to reduced fetal urine output, but this is a late finding and not specific to IUGR. While oligohydramnios often accompanies IUGR, it is not the most accurate diagnostic method.

Once IUGR is diagnosed, a detailed assessment including umbilical artery Doppler studies is required to determine the severity and guide management decisions regarding timing of delivery.
learningPoints: Serial measurements of fetal abdominal circumference is the most sensitive single parameter for diagnosing intrauterine growth restriction.
A single measurement below the 10th centile is insufficient to diagnose intrauterine growth restriction; serial measurements showing declining growth velocity are required.
Fetal abdominal circumference reflects liver size and subcutaneous fat, making it the best indicator of fetal nutrition and growth.
references: Ferrazzi E, Mari G, Rizzo G, Kingdom JC, Kiserud T, Hecher K. Clinical Opinion: The diagnosis and management of suspected fetal growth restriction: an evidence-based approach. Am J Obstet Gynecol. 2022 Mar;226(3):366-378. doi: 10.1016/j.ajog.2021.11.1357. Epub 2022 Jan 10. PMID: 35026129; PMCID: PMC9125563.
